preamble: The transformation from an alveolar click to an alveolar flap is a process of click attrition, where the complex ingressive mechanism is lost, and the alveolar contact resolves into a pulmonic liquid.

examples.note: Historical trends in several Khoisan dialects show the simplification of clicks into alveolar flaps or stops.
Modern variations in urban contact areas can see clicks replaced by pulmonic rhotics or stops in fast speech.

sources: Güldemann, T. (2011). The Macro-Sudan Belt: towards identifying a new linguistic area in Africa.
Miller, A. L. (2007). The Phonetics of Click Consonants.
